content.full_text:
May 24, 1990

Edward Parker  
P.O. Box 140  
Twain, CA 95984

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance  
Our File No. I-90-291

Dear Mr. Parker:

This letter is in response to your questions regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

You are considering running for county office and you have asked how far in advance of an election you may start raising campaign funds.

The Act does not specify when a candidate may begin soliciting contributions. However, if you plan to be a candidate for a California state or local office and intend receive any contributions, you must file with the Fair Political Practices Commission the enclosed Candidate Intention statement (Form 501) before you solicit or receive contributions. (Contributions include loans and use of your personal funds for your campaign.) Upon receiving a contribution, you must establish a campaign bank account at a financial institution in California and file with the Commission the enclosed Campaign Bank Account statement (Form 502) within 24 hours after opening the account.

You have also asked about the campaign reporting requirements and the proper use of campaign funds. The campaign disclosure requirements are contained in the enclosed "1990 Information Manual for Candidates for State and Local Elective Office and Their Controlled Campaign Committees." (See Part VII. beginning on page 26.) The manual also provides information concerning the Act's contribution limits and restrictions on the use of campaign funds. The appropriate disclosure forms are also enclosed.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

Please review the enclosed information manual and if you have any questions, please feel free to call me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Linda Moureaux
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures
